title: Design workflows before prompts.
description: A well-designed workflow makes the prompt almost trivial. Prompts without architecture are just hoping the LLM figures it out.

title: State is a first-class citizen.
description: Every agentic system lives or dies by how it manages state. Treat it as seriously as your database schema.

title: Reliability beats novelty.
description: A system that works 99.9% of the time is worth infinitely more than one that works 80% of the time with impressive benchmarks.

title: Measure before optimizing.
description: Every performance decision must be backed by profiling data. Intuition is a starting point, not a conclusion.

title: Systems over scripts.
description: Scripts solve today's problem. Systems solve a class of problems. Build for the architecture you'll need in 6 months.

title: Every failure is observable.
description: If you can't see it fail, you can't fix it. Instrument everything before it goes to production.

title: Deterministic logic where possible.
description: Use LLMs for reasoning and creativity. Use deterministic code for routing, validation, and state transitions.

title: Build for maintainability.
description: Code you write today is infrastructure someone has to operate tomorrow. Write for the engineer who inherits it.
